albert_park.note: A parkland circuit around Albert Park Lake, reprofiled in 2022 for faster, closer racing.

shanghai.note: The Turn 1–2 'snail' keeps tightening through more than 270 degrees.

suzuka.note: The calendar's only figure-eight — the back straight crosses over the pit straight.

miami.note: A temporary circuit looped around Hard Rock Stadium's campus.

villeneuve.note: The wall on the final chicane's exit is the infamous Wall of Champions.

monaco.note: The slowest, tightest lap of the year — and the shortest circuit on the calendar.

catalunya.note: F1's long-time test track — every driver knows it corner by corner.

red_bull_ring.note: Just ten corners — the shortest lap time of the whole season.

silverstone.note: Hosted the first world-championship grand prix in 1950.

spa.note: Eau Rouge–Raidillon is taken flat out at close to 300 km/h.

hungaroring.note: Tight and twisty — often called 'Monaco without the walls'.

zandvoort.note: The final corner is banked at 18 degrees — steeper than Indianapolis.

monza.note: The Temple of Speed — the highest average lap speed of the season.

madring.note: Brand new for 2026, built around Madrid's IFEMA fairgrounds — no race lap record yet.

baku.note: A 2.2 km flat-out blast past the old city walls closes the lap.

marina_bay.note: F1's first night race, lit by around 1,500 floodlights.

americas.note: Turn 1 sits atop a 40-metre hill built specifically for the circuit.

rodriguez.note: At 2,285 m altitude, the thin air costs the cars about a quarter of their downforce.

interlagos.note: Counter-clockwise and carved into a hillside — the Senna S opens the lap.

vegas.note: Blasts down the Strip past the Sphere at around 340 km/h.

losail.note: Built for MotoGP — fast, flowing, and famously brutal on tyres.

yas_marina.note: The season finale under lights, with a hotel straddling the track.

bahrain.note: A desert circuit that races from dusk into night.

jeddah.note: The fastest street circuit in F1 — 27 corners, most of them taken flat.
